item.title: Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Preface | Table of Contents | Chapter 2: Robert Berry →

Chapter 1#

Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps#

I have been researching the Robert Berry family from Orange County since 1997. When I first started researching, I found that the Census forms from 1790 to 1840, only listed the head of household’s given and surnames. Sometimes they would be listed alphabetically, which was not totally useless but not very helpful either. When the families were listed in the order of farms and plantation physical location, the information became more valuable. I would search the census forms from the very early years and become familiar with as many of the surnames located near one of my known ancestors as possible. Soon I learned that a cluster of surnames in one county would often vary greatly from the surnames listed in the very next county. Since the population was small, clusters of surnames in a close proximity were often related to each other. As our ancestors relocated from one state to another, the pattern was repeated over and over. When I located a family surname who seemed to be a relative from a previous community, I started looking at the surnames of his neighbors. I always paid particular attention to these very close neighbors. This is a very important tool; since there were really not very many people in these early settlements, many of them migrated there together. Many of them were friends and a lot of them were relatives and children who had known each other their entire lives. They had grown up together and when the time came to relocate, they chose to go together.

The Story of David Middleton Berry#

David Middleton Berry was born April 14th, 1805 in Orange County, North Carolina. He died on May 7th, 1880. David married Didama Box about 1826. Didama Box was born in South Carolina on February 14th, 1807. She died on December 14th, 1856 in Fayette County, Alabama. David Middleton Berry married his second wife, Frances E. Henderson on April 15, 1857 in Fayette County, Alabama. Frances was born on January 10th, 1818 in South Carolina and died May 17th, 1885.

The Story of Henry Bradford Berry#

Henry Bradford Berry was born Dec 6, 1829 in Fayette County, Alabama. He married Malissa Jane Jeffries on June 4, 1874. Malissa was born Nov 6, 1851 and died on October 6, 1921. Her headstone is pictured on the right. Henry Bradford Berry’s headstone is on the left. Henry Bradford died on Apr 22, 1895. Henry and Malissa, along with his parents, Thompson and Rosanah were buried in Pleasant Hill Methodist Church Cemetery in Fayette County, Alabama.

The Story of Mary Leona Berry#

Mary Leona Berry was born about 1868 in Alabama and she died on Tuesday April 5, 1949 in Jefferson, Alabama. Mary Leona married Monroe C. Berry on November 5th, 1889. Yes this is a Berry who married a Berry. Monroe was born about 1866 in Alabama and died in 1942 in Jefferson, Alabama.

item.title: Chapter 2: Robert Berry 1729–1814
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys, Kemps | Table of Contents | Chapter 3: Mary Berry →

Chapter 2#

Robert Berry 1729–1814#

Robert Berry OC came to a part of Granville County, North Carolina, which was soon to become Orange County, NC. We know that he built a small cabin in the northeast corner of his staked out land. This cabin was documented in Robert Berry’s OC Entry for his Land Grant. Thomas Bradford owned the land just north of Robert Berry’s OC claim.

item.title: Chapter 4: Robert Berry Jr. 1760–1850
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 3: Mary Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 5: John Berry →

Ancestor Profile: Robert Berry Jr. — view the full family profile on berrytree.org

Chapter 4#

Robert Berry Jr. 1760–before 1850#

Robert Berry Junior was born in Orange County, North Carolina, about 1760 or 1761. He lived in North Carolina until after his parents died. He was listed as a Private in the Revolutionary War (See Fig. 47, Pg. 125) and he listed in the 1790 & 1800 Orange County Census. Robert Berry, Jr., then moved to Person County, NC, and is listed there in the 1810 Census. He was not listed in the 1820 census and listed again in Fayette County, Alabama, in 1830 and 1840 census.

item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors

  4. John Berry (TX)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susanna Aldridge — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL → Texas
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL → Rusk County, TX

Generation 5 John Berry

Born
1815, Orange or Person County, North Carolina
Died
c. 1862, Rusk County, Texas
Married
Mary Howell (married c. 1838, Fayette County, Alabama)
Parents
George Berry and Susanna Aldridge Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

John Berry was born in Orange or Person County, North Carolina in 1815. We are positive that he is a descendant of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry because one of his descendant’s Y-DNA matches the Y-DNA of 13 other known Orange County Berrys. He was first discovered living in Rusk County Texas in the 1860 census but later we found him living in Fayette County, Alabama in 1840. John met and married his wife, Mary Howell in Fayette County, Alabama. They had four children born in Alabama, two daughters and two sons. We have a lot of information on the oldest daughter Sarah Berry but have not located the second daughter. The rest of their children were born in Texas. We have never found an 1850 Census for John and Mary Howell Berry.

Parents
John and Margery Lawrence Cate, Prince George County, Virginia

The Story of Elizabeth Cate Berry#

Elizabeth Cate was the daughter of John and Margery Cate. Her oldest brother was Joshua Cate and her youngest brother was Thomas Cate, who was a chain carrier when Robert Berry’s Granville Grant was surveyed in 1753. Robert Berry and Elizabeth Cate were married about 1759. At least three of their children were already married before marriage records were kept in Hillsborough. The ages of their children would indicate that their marriage took place around 1759.

Parents

The Story of Robert Berry (PAC)#

Robert Berry (PAC) of South Norfolk County, Virginia, married Mary Williamson and established a home in Princess Anne County, Virginia, which is now known as Virginia Beach, Virginia. Robert and Mary had two sons. The oldest was Richard Berry and the baby was Robert Berry “Orange County, NC” (OC) who was born about 1729. Robert Berry (PAC) wrote his will in 1730 and died shortly after Robert Berry (Orange County, NC) was born.

item.title: Aldridge Berry Farquhar Waggoner Connection
item.description:
The Aldridge, Berry, Farquhar & Waggoner Connection

Four families intermarried across multiple generations from Orange County, NC, to Fayette County, Alabama, and Burnet County, Texas.

There are several marriages in different Berry lines involving these four families.

Mattie Lee Berry was born 1882 in Fayette County, Alabama. She married David Loyd Strickland about 1902. In 1910 Mattie Lee Berry Strickland and her family were living with her widowed mother Malissa Jeffries Berry in Holly Springs, Fayette County, Alabama.

item.title: Francis Marion Berry
item.description:
• Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — North Carolina & Fayette County, Alabama
  • Gen 4 George Berry × Susanna Aldridge — North Carolina & Fayette County, Alabama
  • Gen 5 John Berry × Mary Howell — Fayette County, Alabama & Rusk County, Texas
  • Gen 6 Francis Marion Berry × Fannie Lillie Hudspeth John Berry was born in Person County North Carolina in 1815. his parents were George and Susan Aldridge. They were married after George Berry completed his tour of duty in the war of 1812 John Berry was about five years old when he left North Carolina with his father George, and mother Susanna Berry, his grandfather Robert Berry Jr.and his Grandmother Mary Waggoner Berry.

Congress made public lands available in Tuscaloosa Alabama on April 24 1820. Robert Berry Jr. listed in the 1810 Person County Census but it appears he and his family left North Carolina before the 1820 census was taken in Person County

Born
c. 1790, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
Before 1850, Texas
Married
Susanna Aldridge (married c. 1814)
Parents
Robert Berry Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

George Berry was born c. 1790 in Orange County North Carolina. George was killed in Texas before 1850. George had served in the North Carolina Militia in the war of 1812. He enlisted as a private in Capt. John Bradshaw’s Company in the North Carolina Militia. 1814 Muster: 4th Regiment, 7th Company. He was discharged as a private and his service made him eligible for land grants in Fayette County Alabama. George Berry’s service in Texas also made his heirs eligible for more land grants in Texas.

John Berry was born in Orange or Person County, North Carolina in 1815. We are positive that he is a descendant of Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry because one of his descendant’s Y-DNA matches the Y-DNA of 13 other known Orange County Berrys. He was first discovered living in Rusk County Texas in the 1860 census but later we found him living in Fayette County, Alabama in 1840. John met and married his wife, Mary Howell in Fayette County, Alabama. They had four children born in Alabama, two daughters and two sons. We have a lot of information on the oldest daughter Sarah Berry but have not located the second daughter. The rest of their children were born in Texas. We have never found an 1850 Census for John and Mary Howell Berry.

item.description:
Land Grant to Robert Berry Jr.

This land grant certificate was issued by President Monroe to Robert Berry Jr. for property in Alabama.

item.title: Leona Berry Borden (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Leona Berry Borden
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — NC, AL → Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Alexander Howard Berry × Josephine Gregory — Texas
Gen 7Leona Berry × Ben Summers / Dick Simpson — Indian Territory → Borden County, TX

Generation 7 Leona Berry

Born
November 19, 1894, Indian Territory
Married
1st: Ben Summers (m. January 4, 1911; d. 1918) — 2nd: Montgomery 'Dick' Simpson (m. November 27, 1920)
Parents
Alexander Howard Berry and Josephine Priscilla Gregory Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Leona Berry was born on November 19, 1894 in Indian Territory. Leona Berry married Ben Summers Jan 4 1911. He died in the 1917 flu epidemic in Colorado. The family heard there was work in Colorado so the A.H. Berrys, the Willie Berrys with son Alec, daughter Hettie and a second son, Willie Howell Jr., born June 2, 1917, the Ben Summers family with three children, Newborn, born 1913, J.C., born 1915 and Maggie born 1917, the Hal Johnsons and seven children, Myrtle, Edith, Lula, Matthew, Albert, WillEtta, and Leola, and the C.L. Johnsons all loaded into covered wagons and went to Pueblo, Colorado. When they arrived at Pueblo, the Hal Johnson, and Ben Summers families loaded their belongings into boxcars and continued on to Rifle, Colorado. A.H. Berry was not well and decided to continue on in the wagons. He was accompanied by the Willie Berry family. The C.L. Johnson family stayed in Pueblo. Rifle was across the mountains from Pueblo and when A.H. and his group reached Salida, Colorado the altitude was too high and he could not breathe. They backed the wheels of the wagons into the river to turn around as the road was too narrow, and returned to Pueblo. When they arrived there was a message waiting for Willie from Hal Johnson asking them to wait as they were returning on the same train they had gone on. They had unloaded nothing but the horses. After they returned to Pueblo the group found jobs and went to work. In a few months A.H. became homesick and wanted to return to Texas. The A.H. Berrys and the Willie Berrys did so at that time, August 1918, but the Johnsons and Summers families did not return with them. Those who stayed in Colorado all came down with the influenza and Ben Summers died. So many people were dying that it was almost a week before he could be buried in Pueblo. A few months after Ben Summers death, Hal Johnson and his family and Leona (Onie) Berry Summers and her children loaded their livestock and belongings into a boxcar and rode the train back to O’Donnell. That was December 1918. When they returned to Texas they again settled in Borden County. Leona and her three children moved into the house with her parents.

On November 27, 1920 Leona (Onie) Summers married Montgomery (Dick) Simpson in Tahoka, Texas.

item.title: Mary Kempe PAC
item.description:
Mary Kempe of Princess Anne County

Daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe, married Richard Williamson.

Mary Kempe from Princess Ann County Virginia#

PARENTS:

Mary’s Parents George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe

MARY KEMPE married RICHARD WILLIAMSON (daughter of George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe of Kempe’s Landing) There is some question at this time about Mary Kempe’s marriage date since George and Mary Hutchison Kempe may not have arrived in America before 1650. My thoughts are that Richard Williamson and Mary Kempe were probably married around 1670 to 1675. From Richard Williamson’s 1721 will we learn that his children were:

item.title: Proof Page
item.description:
Proof of the Berry-Williamson Connection

Evidence linking Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through the Williamson and Kempe families.

This page assembles the documentary evidence connecting Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through shared surnames, tax lists, and land records.

item.title: Sarah Berry (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Sarah Berry (TX)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — NC, AL → Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Sarah Berry × Hiram Howard / Francis McClure / Robert Taylor / Henry Stoneham / Henrick Dippold — Alabama → Rusk County, TX → Galveston, TX

Generation 6 Sarah Berry

Born
April 20, 1839, Alabama
Died
June 5, 1917, Galveston, Texas
Married
1st: Hiram Howard (m. 1853) — 2nd: Francis M. McClure (m. 1859) — 3rd: Robert A. Taylor (m. 1863) — 4th: Henry B. Stoneham (m. 1868) — 5th: Henrick Dippold (m. 1880)
Parents
John Berry and Mary Howell Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

She was born on April 20, 1839 in Alabama and died in Galveston Texas on June 5th 1917. We were able to find Sarah Berry Dippold’s grave in 2006 while I was staying in Houston for medical reasons. The grave is located in the Trinity Episcopal Church Cemetery which is located in Galveston Texas. She is buried in Block # 100 plot 18 of that Cemetery. I was unable to locate her husband but he may still be buried there. This Cemetery has been raised several times, as it keeps sinking they haul in more dirt. Each family is responsible to see that their relatives headstones are raised to the new level. If the family does not assume that responsibility the grave is covered with dirt and is no longer visible. The sinking of the Island is caused by removal of oil from the surface below. Sarah was married five times. This was during the period of the civil war and may be the reason so many of them died. She married her first husband Hiram Howard on November 24th 1853. Sarah married her second husband, Francis M. McClure on January 5th 1859. On May 18th 1863 Sarah married her third husband Robert A. Taylor. Sarah married her fourth husband, Henry B. Stoneham on August 29 1868. She married her fifth husband Henrick Dippold on February 15th 1880.

Generation 5 Susan Berry

Born
1834, Alabama
Died
After 1880, Gregg County, Texas
Married
Moses Ray (married c. January 1849)
Parents
George Berry and Susan Berry, Fayette County, Alabama
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

SUSAN BERRY was born in 1834 in Alabama. Susan Berry was probably 15 years old when she married Moses Ray. They were most likely married in January 1849. Their first daughter Sarah E Ray was born in November 1849. Sarah E. Ray was listed as eleven months old in October 1850. Susan and Moses had six children. Susan died after 1880 in Gregg Texas.

item.title: Thompson Berry Ala
item.description:
Thompson Berry, Son of Robert Berry Jr. and Mary Camp Berry#

Ancestors:#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Generation Robert Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry NC and Fayette County, Alabama

THOMPSON BERRY was born December 18, 1801 in Orange County, North Carolina and he died in Fayette County, Alabama on January 8, 1871. Thompson Berry married Rosanah Gutman who was born in South Carolina on November 25, 1803 and died on July 17, 1890 in Fayette County, Alabama.

item.title: William Cate's Estate Sale
item.description:
William Cate's Estate Sale — 1803

Items purchased by Robert Berry Sr. and family at the estate sale of their father-in-law, William Cate.

Below is a list of items that Robert Berry Sen, and his family bought at William Cate’s Estate Sale in 1803. William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmitt) Cate were Hannah and Sarah Cates’ parents and William and Thomas Berry’s father and mother-in-law.

item.title: William Thomas Berry Jr. Ala
item.description:
William Thomas Berry Jr.

Son of William Thomas Sr. and Louisa Bertha Minns Berry, Alabama.

William Thomas Berry Jr., son of William Thomas Sr. and Louisa Bertha Minns#

ANCESTORS

  • Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County Virginia.
  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  • Generation Robert Jr and Mary Waggoner Berry; NC and Fayette County, Alabama
  • Generation Thompson and Roseann Gutman Berry: Fayette County Alabama.
  • Generation Henry Bradford and Malissa Jefferies Berry
  • Generation William Thomas Snr. and Lousia Bertha Minns Berry

WILLIAN THOMAS BERRY JR. married Mattie Lee Herrin in Cleveland and they lived in Oakwood Village, Ohio. William died in 1960. William and Mattie Lee had 6 children: Sandra, William T Jr., James, John, David, and Joyce. William T. Jr. and John are both deceased.

owell Berry Jr. was born on June 2nd 1917 in Berry Flat, Borden Texas. He married (Living Wilson.) The picture on the right was taken in Colorado in 1982.
